phrasalVerbs.definition: become successful or popular very fast
examples: The iPod has really taken off over the last couple of years.
As the plane took off, I looked down at the houses below us.
Carl took off his coat and started to warm himself by the fire.
There’s so much work to do in the office that maybe we should take on a secretary.
I haven’t got time to take on any more projects at the moment.
When Mr Barker retired, his daughter took over the family business.
The class took to Mrs Openshaw straight away.
I’ve taken to going for a five-mile run every morning.
Why don’t you take up a hobby, like collecting something?
